message:
test(harness): Track G certification-policy integration tests (bd-2yqp6.7)

Adds the integration test file that exercises the new
`certification_policy` module end to end against the release
certificate generator. These tests are the concrete checks that
keep the Track G thresholds honest across refactors — if any of
the policy constants regress or the release-certificate generator
stops threading the policy through, the bead-tagged failures make
the regression easy to triage.

crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2yqp6_7_certification_policy.rs (216 lines)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
* File-level bead tag `const BEAD_ID: &str = "bd-2yqp6.7"` so every
  assertion can reference the owning Track G bead in its failure
  message, matching the pattern the other harness integration
  tests use.
* Imports from the new module surface:
    - `CERTIFICATION_MAX_EVIDENCE_AGE_HOURS`
    - `CERTIFICATION_MIN_VERIFICATION_PCT`
    - `CERTIFICATION_POLICY_ID`
    - `REQUIRED_CERTIFICATION_LANES`
    - `canonical_certification_policy`
  ...and the full set of release-certificate / parity-catalog /
  drift-monitor / adversarial-campaign / verification-contract
  types required to build a realistic `CertificateInputs` without
  mocking out the gate layer.
* A `passing_contract_outcome()` test helper constructs a
  `ContractEnforcementOutcome` in the `EnforcementDisposition::Pass`
  state with fresh schema version and default counters, so the
  happy-path tests do not each inline the same boilerplate.
* The test bodies (to be extended in follow-up commits) drive the
  release certificate through:
    1. `canonical_certification_policy()` wired into
       `CertificateConfig`.
    2. A canonical parity universe / catalog built via
       `build_canonical_universe` + `build_canonical_catalog`.
    3. A realistic `ArtifactManifest` built from
       `ArtifactEntry` / `ArtifactKind` so the evidence-traceability
       checks exercise the full wiring.
    4. `evaluate_full` + `build_evidence_ledger` against the
       drift monitor snapshot and adversarial campaign result.
    5. `build_certificate` and verdict-level assertions using
       `CertificateVerdict`, with
       `CERTIFICATION_TRACEABILITY_SCHEMA_VERSION` pinned.

No production code changes; this is pure test surface that lets
the Track G certification policy live under version control
alongside its enforcement tests instead of shipping the policy
module without integration coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
